statement.markdown: Given a Trie data structure, implement an insert operation that correctly adds a word to the Trie. The Trie contains a root node and a set of nodes where each node contains a character (string of length 1), a boolean flag indicating whether the node is the end of a word, and pointers to its children. The Trie can be traversed using the character value of each node. The insert operation takes a string of characters as input and should add it to the Trie if it is not already present. Note that duplicate words should not be inserted.
statement.inputFormat: (root node of the Trie, input string of characters)
statement.outputFormat: root node of the Trie with the inserted word
statement.notes: Please implement the insert operation using the provided starter code. Time and space complexity should be O(m), where m is the length of the input string.

examples.input:
root None
a a
b b
c c
examples.explanation: The input string 'abc' should be inserted into the Trie correctly.

examples.input:
root None
a a
b b
c c
abc a
examples.explanation: The input string 'abc' should not be inserted into the Trie because it already exists.

hints.text: Check if the node to be inserted exists in the Trie before performing the insertion.

editorial.title: Correctly Implementing Trie Insert Operation
editorial.intuition: To correctly implement the Trie insert operation, we need to traverse the Trie based on the input string and create new nodes when necessary.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would be to traverse the Trie for each character in the input string and create new nodes when necessary. This approach has a time complexity of O(m^2), where m is the length of the input string.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would be to use a Trie node that contains a character, a boolean flag indicating whether the node is the end of a word, and pointers to its children. We can traverse the Trie based on the input string and create new nodes when necessary. This approach has a time complexity of O(m), where m is the length of the input string.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution would be to use a Trie node that contains a character, a boolean flag indicating whether the node is the end of a word, and pointers to its children. We can traverse the Trie based on the input string and create new nodes when necessary. We should also handle duplicate words correctly by not inserting them into the Trie. This approach has a time complexity of O(m), where m is the length of the input string.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: We can prove the correctness of our solution by showing that it correctly inserts words into the Trie and handles duplicate words correctly.
editorial.dryRun: Let's dry run our solution with the input string 'abc'. We should insert the word 'abc' into the Trie correctly.
commonMistakes: Not traversing the Trie based on the input string

functionSignature.java: public void insertWord(TrieNode root, String word);

testCases.input:
root None
dog a
cat a

root None
dog a
cat a
abc a
